mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 9.0%, it confirms deteriorating labor market conditions that typically precede corporate earnings downgrades, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Joint contraction in industrial production alongside accelerating housing starts signals a sectoral rebalancing that reduces input cost volatility, historically compressing equity risk premiums and lifting broad market indices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The cross-product of normalized INDPRO and HOUST monthly changes yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly from baseline when the interaction term is positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rapid increases in real consumer credit reflect resilient household spending that offsets tighter monetary policy, sustaining equity earnings growth and driving positive forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 210, positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive SPX forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly scales corporate revenue expectations, creating a persistent positive correlation with S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as analyst earnings revisions trend upward.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.10 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls exceed 132,000 thousand, it confirms robust labor demand that typically sustains consumer spending, leading to positive equity returns over a one-month horizon as discount rates stabilize.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 65% of months where PAYEMS exceeds 132,000 thousand over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and rising federal funds rates signal supply-side bottlenecks meeting monetary tightening, which historically compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 50% of instances over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment, accelerating housing starts act as a leading indicator of economic stabilization, triggering a mean-reversion rally in equities over the next week as risk premiums normalize.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 9.2, positive HOUST growth does not produce statistically significant positive SPX 5-day returns (t-stat < 1.96) over a 5-year rolling window.
